home.h1: Bánh tráng trộn that you'll never forget
home.sub: The original Tây Ninh-style spicy rice-paper salad — hand-mixed, bold, and craveable. Eat it once and you'll remember. Ăn là nhớ.
home.point1.d: Made the Tây Ninh way with real shrimp salt, beef floss, peanuts, and herbs.
home.point2.d: Tossed fresh in small batches so every bag has that street-cart flavor.
home.point3.d: Sealed for freshness and shipped fast, with cold-safe snack packaging.
home.featured.sub: Start here — these fly off the shelf.
home.story.t: From a Tây Ninh street cart to your door
home.story.d: “Ăn là nhớ” means “eat it and you'll remember.” We bring the exact bánh tráng trộn we grew up with to snack lovers across America — nothing watered-down, nothing generic.
home.b2b.t: Sell Ăn Là Nhớ in your store or market

about.h1: From a street cart to your door
about.sub: means eat it and you'll remember. We started this because the bánh tráng trộn we grew up with — the real Tây Ninh kind — was nowhere to be found in the US. So we brought it here ourselves.
about.name.d: In Vietnamese, ăn là nhớ means

It's not a marketing line — it's the most honest review bánh tráng trộn has ever received. One bite takes you back: to a school gate in Tây Ninh, to your grandmother's kitchen, to a street corner you haven't visited in years. The snack carries memory in every ingredient.
about.origin.d1: Tây Ninh province, tucked along the Cambodian border northwest of Hồ Chí Minh City, is the undisputed home of bánh tráng trộn. The province has been producing bánh tráng and muối tôm for generations — the local rice paper is thicker and smokier than anything made elsewhere, and the shrimp salt, made from fragrant Vàm Cỏ river shrimp, has a depth of flavor that can't be replicated.
about.origin.d2: When people in other cities say their bánh tráng trộn is
they mean it's the best style. We don't imitate it — we source from there directly.
about.mission.d1: A lot of bánh tráng trộn sold in the US is a compromise — generic rice paper, imitation shrimp salt, simplified recipes. It's fine. But it's not what we grew up eating.
about.mission.d2: Ăn Là Nhớ exists to close that gap. We work directly with Tây Ninh producers to source authentic bánh tráng and muối tôm. We mix in small batches by hand, seal each bag immediately for freshness, and ship across the US within 1–2 business days. Nothing is compromised to make the logistics easier.
about.values.h: Three things we never compromise on
about.v1.d: We source every ingredient from Tây Ninh or from suppliers who work the same way Tây Ninh producers do. If we can't get the real thing, we don't make it.
about.v2.d: Every bag is mixed by hand in small batches. There's no machine that can distribute the ingredients the way a person can — each piece of rice paper deserves attention.
about.v3.d: We exist to serve Vietnamese communities in the US who miss the taste of home — and to introduce one of Vietnam's greatest snacks to anyone who hasn't tried it yet.
about.who.d: We're a Vietnamese-American family business based in the United States, with deep roots in Tây Ninh province. Our founders grew up eating bánh tráng trộn from street carts, and started Ăn Là Nhớ because the diaspora deserved better than the imitations available here.
about.who.d2: We handle everything: sourcing, mixing, packaging, and fulfillment. We work with a small team and we know every bag that goes out the door. If you ever have a question — about an order, about the ingredients, about a wholesale partnership — reach out directly. We actually read the emails.
about.cta.h: The best way to understand us is to taste it.
about.cta.d: Authentic Tây Ninh bánh tráng trộn, sealed fresh and shipped to your door. Ăn là nhớ.
